108.status: Removed from April 25 game after taking a foul tip off his left wrist. Will start a rehab assignment in the Arizona Complex League on May 11.
Struck out seven over 3 1/3 scoreless innings in May 3 rehab start with Single-A Rancho Cucamonga. Allowed three runs over five innings on May 8 with Triple-A Salt Lake.
Exited in the fifth inning on May 6 after injuring himself while trying to beat out an infield single. Requires further evaluation after testing revealed plantar fasciitis.
Pitched four simulated innings with 60 pitches on April 30. Threw five innings in an Arizona Complex League game on May 5 and will start a rehab assignment with Single-A Rancho Cucamonga on May 10.

Began rehab assignment with Single-A Rancho Cucamonga on April 29 by throwing a scoreless inning. Threw another scoreless inning with the Quakes on May 2, allowed one run in one inning on May 5 and will pitch again on May 9.
Left his outing before the start of the third inning on April 29. Underwent an MRI, will be shut down for 3-4 weeks before starting a throwing program.
Was evaluated by Dr. Keith Meister in Texas and underwent season-ending ligament and flexor tendon repair surgery, the club announced April 8.
Not going to play in 2026 and had the last year of his contract deferred over several years. Was placed on 60-day IL on March 24.

110.status: Began third rehab assignment on May 7 and has gone 0-for-4 with three walks in two games for Double-A Chesapeake. Previously played 14 rehab games from March 27-April 21.
Imaging revealed low-grade strain. Began rehab assignment with Double-A Chesapeake on May 9.
Exited May 7 start in Miami due to discomfort in left forearm. Could receive cortisone shot, though other treatment options remain on table.
Dealing with a flu that has circulated around Orioles' clubhouse. Should be ready to go at end of IL stint. Joined team in Miami on May 5.
After testing, received platelet-rich plasma injection on Feb. 20. Shut down from baseball activities on May 4 after experiencing discomfort during throwing progression.
Imaging came back clean. Taking anti-inflammatory medication. Began treatment May 4 and will be re-evaluated for throwing later in the week.
Injury was sustained in days after April 18 start in Cleveland. Expected to miss at least several weeks.
Got second opinion with Dr. Neal ElAttrache in Los Angeles on April 20. Has inflammation in shoulder joint. Felt soreness coming out of March 31 outing.
Exited April 11 game vs. Giants after stumbling between first and second and sliding awkwardly into the bag on a double. Broke fourth metatarsal on left foot. Expected to miss substantial time.
Exited 2026 debut on March 31 due to right elbow discomfort. After getting imaging and a second opinion, underwent Tommy John surgery on April 8.
Underwent surgery to repair torn rotator cuff and torn labrum last Aug. 19. Threw for first time March 3, tossing 25 pitches from 45 feet on flat ground.
Placed on the 60-day injured list on Feb. 14. No clear timeline for potential return.

111.status: Threw out to 100 feet on May 8 and was encouraged by how he felt. Team will have better idea of timetable when he gets off mound.
Imaging on right elbow revealed no structural damage. Resumed playing catch on May 9.
The plan is for the lefty to avoid the IL and return against his former team, the Phillies, at some point during the May 12-14 series.

111.expectedReturn: First day he is eligible to return on May 17
111.status: Has been dealing with neck stiffness that has limited his ability to look directly at plate when pitching. Should be fine once IL status is up.
Underwent additional testing after being recalled from his Minor League rehab assignment on April 20, and imaging revealed nothing alarming.

111.injury: Ruptured left patellar tendon/left abdominal strain
111.status: Rehab from left knee surgery went well, but now has left abdominal strain that will prolong return to action.
Consultation with Dr. Keith Meister revealed right flexor strain. Shut down from throwing for six weeks. Transferred to 60-day IL on April 14.

111.injury: Torn ulnar collateral ligament right elbow/right flexor pronator strain
111.status: Started gradual throwing program on flat ground in the second week of Spring Training.

112.status: Removed in ninth inning on April 23. Bullpen session on May 8 went well. Per manager Craig Counsell, Thielbar will throw off a mound again May 10. From there, live BP and then a Minor League rehab stint would be the next steps.
Had a setback after bullpen session on May 7, per manager Craig Counsell. Dealing with stress reaction near triceps/shoulder. Shut down for a month and has moved rehab to Arizona.
Injured left knee in fluke accident at home on May 6. Had successful left knee meniscectomy surgery on May 7 in Chicago. Expected to miss roughly six weeks.
Threw 75 pitches in 3 2/3 innings (six runs allowed) in fourth rehab outing with Triple-A Iowa on May 3.

Felt discomfort in recent bullpen session. Checkup with Dr. Keith Meister (who did Steele's UCL revision repair on April 18, 2025) showed a flexor strain. Shut down from throwing until asymptomatic, and will be rechecked in a month.
Felt some discomfort in April 23 outing and did not recover well. Further testing showed a flexor strain; timetable estimated to be around eight weeks.

112.playerName: Jaxon Wiggins (Cubs' No. 1 prospect, No. 50 overall)
112.status: Last pitched on April 4 for Iowa. Moved to Minor League IL out of caution. Has resumed playing catch, per president of baseball operations Jed Hoyer.
Underwent reconstruction of ulnar collateral ligament with an internal brace April 20 via Dr. Keith Meister. Initial timetable for return is 12-14 months.
Underwent a right UCL revision repair with an internal brace on April 16. Timetable for return is estimated around 15-16 months.
Underwent a right patellar tendon debridement on Feb. 24. Could miss months while recovering.
Underwent surgery to repair a torn right UCL and flexor tendon last October. Signed a two-year deal with Chicago on Feb. 16 with an eye toward contributing in 2027.

113.status: Took 50 swings in cage on May 7. Backing off from swinging but taking grounders and throwing and working on core exercises with strength coach Rob Fumagalli.
Tossed a scoreless inning with one walk on May 8 against Nashville. Will throw “an inning-plus” on May 12 for Triple-A Louisville.
MRI exam on May 8 showed no structural damage, and he could throw a side session on May 10, per manager Terry Francona.
MRI exam showed a Grade 2 strain, with estimated time away to be four to eight weeks. Starting throwing from his knees on May 8.
Throwing out to 150 feet in Arizona. First bullpen session could happen in late May.
Went to Goodyear, Ariz., to begin rehab process on May 5. Moved to the 60-day IL on May 6, but there was no setback.

114.status: Threw bullpen on May 8. If he feels well coming out of that, will pitch in rehab game in Minor Leagues as next step.
Returned from rehab assignment due to viral illness that caused him not to pitch since April 24. Will begin new rehab stint once back to full strength.
Manager Stephen Vogt noted on May 8 that Arias' rehab is "going to be a little bit longer. He hit a little snag." Was initially expected to miss four to eight weeks.

115.status: Twisted the ankle at Philadelphia on May 8; he reported that it is stiff and sore, but he does not expect it to affect his next appearance.

Will throw a "full-go" bullpen session on May 8, after he had thrown a lighter bullpen on May 5. Will face hitters in Pittsburgh early next week before embarking on a Minor League injury rehab assignment.
Allowed two runs on three hits with two strikeouts in one inning for High-A Spokane in his first injury rehab appearance on April 29.

115.expectedReturn: To be determined after he is cleared for game action
115.status: Continues playing catch at the team's facility in Scottsdale, Ariz.

Underwent ligament transfer Tommy John surgery and internal brace procedure on March 4.
There is no return in sight for Bryant, who has had a long and painful time since signing with the Rockies before the 2022 season.

116.status: Threw a two-inning, 38-pitch simulated game on May 9 in Kansas City. Will throw another, longer sim game next week.

Tossed three perfect innings (27 pitches, 22 strikes, 5 strikeouts) in second rehab start for Single-A Lakeland May 8. Threw more in the bullpen afterwards. Fastball at 95.4 mph, topped at 96.4. Next rehab start likely at High-A or Triple-A.
Tossed a hitless inning with a walk and no strikeouts in second rehab start for Single-A Lakeland May 9. Threw 16 pitches, nine strikes. Fastball averaged 95.4 miles per hour, topped out at 96.4.
Underwent surgery May 6 to remove loose body from elbow. Will report to Lakeland for initial stages of rehab, but will not throw for at least the next few days.
Received cortisone injection May 8 to remove remaining inflammation in forearm. Will resume throwing soon.
Threw latest bullpen session on May 8.
Tossed a scoreless inning on one hit with two strikeouts in his first rehab appearance for Single-A Lakeland on May 8.
Threw a bullpen session on May 8. Will need to face hitters in a simulated game or rehab assignment before he rejoins Tigers.
Left May 2 game with discomfort after sliding into home plate. Tests showed no structural damage, but he is still feeling some soreness.
Completing rehab daily after receiving a cortisone injection in mid-April.

116.injury: Concussion, fractured left radius bone in arm
116.status: Completing rehab daily after undergoing surgery on April 13 to repair radial fracture in left forearm following an outfield collision on April 9.

117.status: Experienced soreness during his throwing program on May 8. Sent home to Houston to see doctors.
Sustained season-ending injury while taking batting practice May 5. Manager Joe Espada said Correa’s surgery will happen soon, but he did not have the exact date.
Ran bases, played defense and hit on the field May 8. Could go out on a Minor League rehab assignment within the next couple of days.
Started throwing program by playing catch up to 120 feet. Tossed a bullpen session on May 8 that went well. Was transferred to 60-day IL on May 9 in a procedural move.
Will return to Houston rotation during May 11-14 series against Seattle after giving up five walks, one run and one hit and striking out three in a Triple-A rehab start May 5.
Trammell is running, hitting and throwing and could be nearing a Minor League rehab assignment.
Was scratched prior to the May 4 game against the Dodgers after suffering an injury swinging the bat in BP and placed on IL.
In first rehab outing May 5 for Triple-A Sugar Land, tossed a scoreless inning, allowing one walk with a strikeout and hitting 94.7 mph.
In first Minor League rehab appearance May 5 for Triple-A Sugar Land, threw two-thirds of an inning and allowed three runs (one earned) with three walks and two strikeouts (34 pitches).
Performing light baseball activities as he prepares to start a running program.
Has begun light baseball activity after exiting the game against the Rockies on April 8 mid-plate appearance following a swing.

117.status: Has progressed to throwing in the bullpen in West Palm Beach after undergoing Tommy John surgery to repair the ulnar collateral ligament with an internal brace as well as a repair of the flexor tendon in his forearm on June 6, 2025.

117.injury: Torn ulnar collateral ligament in right elbow
117.status: Has progressed to throwing in the bullpen in West Palm Beach after undergoing Tommy John surgery to repair the ulnar collateral ligament in his right elbow on May 23, 2025.

117.injury: Torn ulnar collateral ligament in left elbow
117.status: Underwent Tommy John surgery on Sept. 23, 2025.

118.status: Exited May 6 start after three innings and diagnosed with "pitcher's elbow." Shut down until May 10 or 11 before ramping up again.
Suffered injury during first rehab outing on May 6 and will be shut down for three weeks. Returned from rehab assignment May 8.
Threw 26 pitches (14 strikes) in his seventh rehab appearance with Triple-A Omaha on May 6, pitching a scoreless inning with two strikeouts and a walk.
Underwent left labral repair surgery on April 28, ending his season.
After missing 2025 season with an elbow injury, has continued to have problems with soreness. Scheduled to see a specialist to determine next steps.
After missing 2025 with shoulder issues, underwent right labral surgery in October. Rehabbing in Surprise, Ariz., during Spring Training but likely out for year.

119.status: After completing his rehab from September 2025 right shoulder surgery, Stewart made just two appearances for the Dodgers before landing back on the IL.
Exited his May 6 start after one inning with lower back pain. An MRI revealed "nothing really significant," per manager Dave Roberts.
Went 1-for-3 and played five innings at shortstop in the first game of his rehab assignment with Triple-A Oklahoma City on May 8.
Working through a throwing program at the Dodgers' complex in Arizona.
Began a Minor League rehab assignment on May 2. He will likely take close to the maximum 30 days allowed as he builds up his arm.
Tweaked his side while throwing in the bullpen on March 13. He will not begin a throwing program until he's pain-free.
Had surgery on his elbow on April 22. He thinks he will be able to start playing catch a couple weeks after the stitches are removed from his elbow.
Imaging revealed inflammation, but not structural damage, in his shoulder. Has started a throwing program and essentially has an entire Spring Training ramp-up ahead.
Underwent Tommy John surgery in June 2025 and could be a second-half option for the Dodgers.
Progressed to throwing off the mound by the end of Spring Training, but the team is building him up slowly.
Started throwing full bullpen sessions in mid-March.

120.status: Signed to a one-year Major League deal with a club option for the 2027 season on May 5. Reported to West Palm Beach training facility on May 7.
Played catch at a distance of 90 feet, threw off the mound (15 pitches). Will continue throwing progression.
Threw another 30-pitch bullpen session on May 6 at the Nationals’ training complex in West Palm Beach, Fla.
Threw a 30-pitch bullpen session on May 6.
Threw a 25-pitch bullpen session on May 6.
Played catch on May 2, will continue throwing progression at Nationals' training complex.
Continuing his throwing progression at the Nationals' training complex in West Palm Beach, Fla.
Continuing his throwing progression at the Nationals' training complex in West Palm Beach, Fla.
Underwent Tommy John surgery and internal brace procedure on April 20 to repair flexor tendon and UCL..

121.injury: Left lat surgery, left hip discomfort
121.status: Was pulled off his rehab assignment May 6 after complaining of left hip discomfort. Will refrain from throwing for several days before restarting his progression, which will include a fresh 30-day rehab clock.
Restarted a throwing program May 6, nine days after receiving an epidural. Had been experiencing back discomfort since Spring Training, but did not report it until after an April 26 start.
Developed a blister during an April 13 appearance in Los Angeles. Started a rehab assignment on April 25 and has made four appearances.
Fractured his thumb sliding into first base in a May 2 loss to the Angels. Will miss 6-8 weeks.

Wrist soreness popped up April 14 in addition to his Achilles discomfort. Has been performing light baseball activities, including batting practice on the field, but remains in a week-to-week progression.
Received an injection April 30 and will rest for seven to 10 days. He will be re-evaluated then to determine his potential return date.
Strained calf April 22. Will be in a boot for a week and undergo a follow-up MRI the week of May 11. Will remain on a fluid timeline until he receives results.
Began feeling knee discomfort during an April 12 game against the Athletics. An April 14 MRI revealed a meniscus tear, requiring surgery. Had stitches removed the week of April 20.
Exited the Mets' March 21 game after four innings due to knee discomfort and underwent an MRI, which revealed the tear. Will spend his recovery on the Minor League IL.
Fractured a rib during a March 5 game against the Nationals. Will need 4-8 weeks to recover, after which he can commence his buildup.
Will miss all of 2026 recovering from surgery but is under team control through 2029.
Will miss all of 2026 recovering from surgery but is under team control through 2027.
Will miss all of 2026 recovering from surgery but is under team control through 2029.

133.injury: Mid foot bone bruise in right foot
133.status: Has begun a ground-based running progression and hitting progression. No timetable for return.
Still being held out of all baseball activities at this time. No timetable for return.
Began a throwing progression April 19. No timetable for his return.

134.status: Felt slight tugging in his left leg prior to the Pirates' game on May 7. Injury does not appear to be serious.
Placed on the 15-day injured list after serving a two-game MLB suspension for throwing at Cincinnati’s Sal Stewart in a May 2 game.
Allowed one run on five hits in three innings in a rehab start May 6 for Triple-A Indianapolis. Fastball touched 99 mph. Threw 54 pitches, walking one and striking out two.

135.status: Underwent surgery in October 2024. Was hopeful for an early season return but experienced a setback after making one start in Spring Training. Has played light catch but has recently been shut down entirely.
Experienced concussion symptoms after a fastball off the chin on April 18. Initially cleared protocols, but symptoms arose in the days afterward. Has not resumed baseball activity.
Fouled a ball off his toe on May 5, sustaining what the team said was a small fracture. No clear timeline for a return, but absence is not expected to be extensive.
Landed on the IL after a poor start on May 1. Will take several days to rest and recover before beginning a throwing program.

Exited April 12 start with elbow stiffness; shut down from throwing. MRI revealed flexor strain. Expected to pitch again this season, though manager Craig Stammen noted Pivetta's absence would last "weeks and maybe months."
Underwent surgery to repair the flexor tendon in his right elbow on March 17 and will miss the 2026 season.

Underwent an internal brace procedure on his right elbow on Oct. 29, which comes with a recovery timetable of 12-15 months. Will miss the 2026 season and was placed on the restricted list on March 25.
Underwent UCL internal brace surgery as well as a flexor tendon repair operation in April 2025. Resumed throwing bullpens in Padres camp.

136.status: Set to come off the injured list and make start in Houston on May 13. Made his fourth rehab start on May 6 at High-A Everett and threw 61 pitches.
Played catch for the first time since the issue first surfaced, on May 5. MRI revealed "trace inflammation" but no structural damage.More »
Began rehab assignment at Triple-A Tacoma on May 5. Suffered the injury during brief callup from April 14-16 in San Diego.
Placed on injured list on May 4, five days after his most recent appearance.
Began a rehab assignment at Triple-A Tacoma on May 3, which will at minimum last seven days. Final hurdle to clear is throwing to the plate, along with live at-bats.
Placed on the IL before May 2 game vs. Royals.
Injured the other (left) groin during a rehab assignment and was shut down on April 18. Will be reassessed in mid-to-late May to begin a fresh rehab assignment.
Experienced setback during early stages of throwing program and will now have to begin fresh once he's cleared to throw again.
Underwent surgery to repair his right ulnar collateral ligament in January, and will be out all season.More »

137.status: Exited May 5 start after throwing only 62 pitches.
Began a rehab assignment with Triple-A Sacramento on May 5.
Scheduled to begin a rehab assignment with Triple-A Sacramento on May 10.
Playing catch out to 75 feet and has started a swing progression.
Began a rehab assignment with Triple-A Sacramento on May 7. Served as the DH on May 8 and is expected to catch on May 10.
Gave up two runs over nine innings in his first nine rehab appearances with Triple-A Sacramento.
Scheduled to begin a rehab assignment with Single-A San Jose on May 10.
Scheduled to throw live batting practice on May 9 in Arizona.
Underwent a second surgery to repair an axillary artery in his upper right arm on April 17, which will sideline him for five to six months.
Underwent season-ending Tommy John surgery with Dr. Keith Meister on March 25.
Expected to miss 12-14 months after undergoing Tommy John surgery on Sept. 24, 2025.
Expected to miss the entire 2026 season after undergoing Tommy John surgery in November 2025.

138.status: Hit on the left leg by a 97.5 mph fastball from Brandon Sproat on May 6. Stayed in the game for another inning before being replaced defensively.
Manager Oliver Marmol described that Urías has dealt with elbow discomfort on and off dating back to spring. Team will shut him down for multiple weeks before re-evaluating.
Pitched a scoreless inning vs. Indianapolis on May 3 as he continues a rehab assignment with Triple-A Memphis.
Expected to begin rehab assignment around May 10, per Cardinals manager Oliver Marmol. Eligible to come off injured list May 24.

139.injury: Right shoulder impingement and ankle sprain
139.status: Sprained ankle running to first base April 11 on rehab assignment. Resumed assignment with Triple-A Durham on April 24. Has been playing regularly and could make his Rays debut in Boston.

Felt soreness in the sixth inning of his May 3 start, then underwent an MRI on May 4 that revealed inflammation. Imaging showed no structural issues, so he should resume throwing after a brief period of rest.
Signed to a Major League contract on April 14 and immediately placed on the IL. Scheduled to face hitters for the first time the weekend of May 8-10, with a rehab assignment to follow.

Imaging revealed no structural damage. Faced hitters in live BP April 30, threw a bullpen session May 3 and departed for Triple-A Durham to begin a rehab assignment. Will need time to get built up as a starter.
Did not pitch all spring while dealing with a back issue that required an injection. Also underwent an appendectomy. Will need time to build up after being shut down so long.

Set for season-ending surgery on May 13. Said multiple exams revealed "normal" wear and tear on labrum and femoroacetabular impingement, which limited his leg's range of motion. Expecting a six-month recovery followed by a full offseason and plenty of time to be ready for Spring Training.
Sustained a Grade 2 left ankle sprain while running the bases for Triple-A Durham on April 22. Imaging revealed no fracture.

After rocky rehab outing on April 15, he reported soreness in back of right shoulder. Further evaluation revealed a new injury, a subscapularis strain. Shut down for 2-3 weeks, meaning he's likely 4-6 weeks from returning.
Underwent surgery to repair a right elbow flexor strain on Aug. 8. Hopes to return in June or July.

140.status: Had a setback in his throwing program, but began throwing from 60 feet during the Rangers’ most recent homestand and has been cleared to begin his progression again.
Reached 97 mph in one inning of a rehab appearance with Triple-A Round Rock on April 23 but experienced a setback and has been shut down.
Smith played all nine innings on May 3 against the Tigers. Ezequiel Duran will likely get the bulk of the time at second base while Smith is out.
Langford flew from Arizona to join the team in Detroit on May 2, and will not begin ramping up his swing for at least a week, focusing on cardio work and conditioning until cleared to hit.
Examined by team doctors on April 20 for shoulder soreness and was placed on the IL on April 23.
Only threw one inning in an April 14 loss to the A’s due to arm fatigue that was eventually classified as shoulder impingement.
Left Tuesday night’s loss to the A’s after throwing two pitches due to feeling something in his arm. Scans showed minimal issues, though he will be down for at least two weeks.
Down four to six weeks minimum before re-evaluation.
Signed to a Major League deal on Feb. 13, though is continuing to rehab from Tommy John surgery. Should be back on a big league mound around the Trade Deadline.

141.status: Scheduled to meet with Dr. Keith Meister on May 12 after a recent MRI showed inflammation and a "small change" to his injury.

141.status: Received cortisone injection to his forearm on May 7 and will be shut down from throwing for five days.
Scheduled to throw another live BP as he continues to ramp up.
Has started swinging a bat and remains on track for his original projected timeline.
Has started running at the club's Florida complex and will continue to progress.
Expected to pitch in rehab games with FCL Blue Jays beginning on May 8 as he continues to build up in Dunedin, Fla.
Remains in Florida. Manager John Schneider did not have an immediate update on his throwing status but mentioned he would be checking on it.
Discovered after Estrada was optioned, so he has been placed on the Major League IL.
Underwent surgery and is expected to miss the rest of 2026.
Underwent surgery to repair the labral tear and is hoping to return to the Blue Jays in five to six months. This timeline should be clearer by June, but any return in 2026 would be a success.
Underwent surgery and will miss the entire 2026 season.

142.status: Soreness popped up after his start against the Nationals on May 5. Underwent a clean MRI and is set to get a second opinion from Dr. Keith Meister.
Experienced renewed elbow soreness after last bullpen session and received cortisone injection in his triceps. Will be re-evaluated when team returns home May 12.
Threw bullpen session on May 6 and is expected to take his turn as scheduled on May 9 in Cleveland.
Had been considered day to day, but was placed on the injured list with a muscle strain in his thumb.
Suffered renewed discomfort after his third live batting practice session and is being evaluated for next steps.
Threw a bullpen session May 2, and next step will be another bullpen. Targeting mid-May for a rehabilitation assignment.
Felt discomfort while warming up on April 30 and was placed on IL following MRI. Strain is considered to be mild.

142.status: Underwent surgery on Feb. 25 to repair, rather than reconstruct, the UCL and will miss the season.More >
Placed on IL on April 28. Last pitched on April 25, allowing one run and striking out two.

143.status: Miller was fielding ground balls in April but is no longer doing baseball activities. It might be a setback, although some say the Phillies are being cautious with his recovery. His progress has been slow, and the Phillies have declined to delve into details about it.

He pitched five times in seven days from April 19-25, including Rob Thomson’s last win as Phillies manager on April 25.
He is playing catch. He felt something in his calf while doing agility work on April 14.

144.status: Did agility drills in the outfield before the May 8 game at Dodger Stadium. Might not be ready to be activated when eligible on May 13, but could rejoin lineup when next road trip begins (May 18).

144.injury: Torn tendon in right middle finger
144.status: Went 1-for-3 for Triple-A Gwinnett on May 8; is 6-for-20 with no extra-base hits in seven rehab games. Could be activated during May 12-17 homestand.
Felt discomfort after his April 21 appearance. Began a rehab assignment with Triple-A Gwinnett on May 5.
Could be available at some point after the All-Star break.
Underwent arthroscopic surgery on Feb. 23 to have loose bodies removed from his right elbow. Could return in June or July, but the team isn’t putting a timetable on it.
Underwent surgery on his right elbow on June 9.
Will miss the 2026 season after tearing his right ACL during a collision at first base during a Grapefruit League game on March 8.
Underwent arthroscopic surgery to remove bone spurs from his elbow on Feb. 18. There is a chance he could return in June or July, but the team isn’t putting a timetable on it.
Missed 2025 while recovering from left knee surgery and he had a cleanup procedure in November. There is no timetable for his return.

145.status: Took live batting practice on May 9 and will be testing his baserunning all weekend. If all goes well, he could start an injury rehab assignment with Triple-A Charlotte on May 12.
Continues with the bullpen portion of his recovery ramp-up, having thrown seven. Has felt great as the rehab process moves forward.
Hays had just come back from an IL stint due to a Grade 1 right hamstring strain before first feeling this injury on the off-day of April 30.
The White Sox Opening Day starter left his last start for Triple-A Charlotte on April 30 due to the shoulder issue. It’s the first time Smith has dealt with this malady this season.
Manager Will Venable wasn’t sure when Pereira suffered the injury during an April 28 win over the Angels. But it was bothering Pereira both hitting and throwing.
Underwent season-ending Tommy John surgery with the procedure performed by Dr. Keith Meister, but was with the team for the home opener.
Placed on 60-day IL as he continues to recover from Tommy John surgery he underwent last year. Has thrown numerous times off the mound.
Underwent successful surgery to repair the torn ACL on Oct. 28. Estimated recovery time is eight months, per the team.

146.status: Has thrown two bullpens. Will throw live batting practice on May 10 and travel with the club to Minnesota. Exited April 27 outing after experiencing diminished sensation in pitching hand.

146.playerName: Kevin Defrank (Marlins No. 5 prospect, No. 98 overall)
146.status: No surgery required but also no timeline for a return.

According to manager Clayton McCullough, Conine is running at 70 percent, is hitting and throwing. Trending toward a quicker timeline of return. Underwent left hamstring tendon excision week of April 13.

146.injury: UCL reconstruction with an internal brace (hybrid)
146.status: Underwent surgery, which has a recovery period of 13-14 months, on March 11. Experienced the discomfort on a pitch during his March 2 outing.

146.injury: Right elbow UCL reconstruction surgery with an associated internal brace
146.status: Underwent the hybrid procedure in December.

147.status: Scratched from his May 7 start due to a viral infection. Threw a bullpen session May 8 in Milwaukee and is scheduled to rejoin rotation May 11 in Baltimore.
Threw 4 1/3 innings in fourth rehab start on May 5 for High-A Hudson Valley. Scheduled to make fifth start on May 10 for Double-A Somerset. Will likely need at least two more.
Left the April 24 game after feeling tightness in calf. Underwent MRI on April 27. Has not resumed running as of May 5, and has since returned home to practice hitting.
Injured shoulder while crashing into wall on a catch attempt May 7. Placed in concussion protocol but is not showing concussion symptoms, per manager Aaron Boone.
Placed on IL while pitching with Triple-A Scranton/Wilkes-Barre. Will not throw for at least three weeks.
Made third rehab start on May 5 with Triple-A Scranton/Wilkes-Barre. Expected to come of injured list and make 2026 MLB debut May 10 in Milwaukee.

Will not throw for approximately three weeks. Manager Aaron Boone said there is “acute and chronic stuff going on there” that will require rest. Experienced difficulty while throwing on April 25 in Houston.
Threw 10 pitches from a mound April 10, his first time doing so since Tommy John surgery in July 2025. Could face hitters in June and is on track for MLB activation sometime in August.

158.status: Slid into a concrete section of the wall while chasing a foul ball on May 8 against the Yankees. X-rays were negative, but he suffered a deep laceration that will require additional testing scheduled for May 11. (last updated: May 9)
Participated in baserunning drills on May 8 and has a chance to be back in the Brewers' lineup during the current homestand, which runs through May 14. (last updated: May 8)

Exited an April 30 start complaining of a "dead arm" sensation. Traveled to Dallas for a hands-on assessment with Dr. Keith Meister on May 5 and had fluid drained. Rejoined the team on May 8 and will resume throwing on May 9. (last updated: May 8)

Returned from rehab assignment on May 4 after three poor outings but has continued throwing bullpen sessions. "I wanted to work on things and take a step back and really recover to a spot where we can hit the ground running next time out," said Priester, who expects to face hitters in a simulated game on May 11. Scheduled for season-ending Tommy John surgery on May 11. (last updated: May 6)
